mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.5% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above 290, the Fed is in active tightening mode. This constrains equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced corporate profit margins. S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days turn negative as market reprices growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:As of 2022-10-15, FEDFUNDS=3.08 and CPIAUCSL=298.062, indicating active monetary tightening
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when trigger condition is met, or Rank IC between (FEDFUNDS, CPIAUCSL) interaction and forward returns falls below 0.05 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration and rising recession risk. Equity investors reduce risk exposure in anticipation of earnings compression. S&P 500 forward returns decline over the subsequent 60 trading days as the market reprices terminal growth rates downward.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines by more than 200,000 persons over 3 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:As of 2022-10-15, PAYEMS=153,308 thousand, near cycle highs
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following payroll declines exceed +2% in more than 50% of triggered instances, or correlation between 3-month PAYEMS change and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly measure consumer demand strength. When both decline simultaneously by more than 5% over 2 months, it signals demand destruction and inventory correction. Equity investors anticipate margin compression and reduced earnings guidance. S&P 500 forward returns turn negative over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 5% AND HOUST declines > 5% over 2-month window
evidence_refs: fact:As of 2022-09-15, RSAFS=683,974 million and HOUST=1,439 thousand
regime_hint: demand-side recession risk with synchronized consumer and housing weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances when both RSAFS and HOUST decline jointly, or the interaction term (RSAFS × HOUST) has Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) is a high-frequency proxy for real economic capacity utilization. When INDPRO declines for two consecutive months, it signals supply-side weakness and potential stagflation risk. Equity investors reduce growth expectations. S&P 500 forward returns decline over 5 trading days as the market reprices near-term earnings revisions.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:As of 2022-09-15, INDPRO=105.1762, reflecting recent manufacturing softness
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances following consecutive INDPRO declines, or Rank IC between INDPRO momentum and 5-day SPX returns is below 0.03 over any 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) is a lagging indicator of labor market health. When UNRATE rises above 4.0%, it signals labor market slack and reduced wage pressure. Equity investors interpret this as a signal that the Fed may pause or cut rates in the medium term, supporting equity valuations. S&P 500 forward returns turn positive over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:As of 2022-10-15, UNRATE=3.7%, below the 4.0% threshold
regime_hint: late-cycle labor market transition toward Fed pivot
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE > 4.0%, or Rank IC between UNRATE level and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is below 0.04 over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) and the federal funds rate interact to determine the real policy rate. When GDPC1 growth slows below 1.5% annualized while FEDFUNDS remains above 3.0%, the real rate becomes restrictive. Equity investors anticipate earnings downgrades and reduced terminal growth. S&P 500 forward returns decline over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:As of 2022-07-15, GDPC1=20,021.721 billion (2017 dollars), with recent growth deceleration; FEDFUNDS=3.08%
regime_hint: restrictive monetary policy during growth deceleration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 58% of instances when real rate is restrictive, or the interaction term (GDPC1 growth × FEDFUNDS) has Rank IC < 0.05 over any 16-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) lead consumer spending and employment by 6-12 months. A sustained decline in HOUST below 1,400 thousand units signals weakening future demand and employment. Equity investors anticipate earnings downgrades in cyclical sectors. S&P 500 forward returns decline over 60 trading days as the market reprices long-term growth.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1,400 thousand units and remains there for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:As of 2022-09-15, HOUST=1,439 thousand, near the 1,400 threshold
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns exceed +1.5% in more than 60% of instances when HOUST < 1,400, or Rank IC between HOUST level and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is below 0.04 over any 20-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is elevated above 295 and industrial production (INDPRO) growth slows below 0.5% month-over-month, it signals stagflation risk. Equity investors reduce valuations due to margin compression and reduced real growth. S&P 500 forward returns decline over 20 trading days as the market reprices terminal multiples downward.
evidence_refs: fact:As of 2022-10-15, CPIAUCSL=298.062 and INDPRO=105.1762, consistent with stagflation risk
regime_hint: stagflation regime with persistent inflation and supply weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 57% of instances when stagflation conditions are met, or the interaction term (CPIAUCSL × INDPRO growth) has Rank IC < 0.05 over any 14-month rolling window.
